title: Pass 5.B.4 follow-up: wire FusedChain PTX runtime executor (jit ‚Üî gpu integration; route apply_fused on CUDA)
description:
Spun off from #1106 (Pass 5.B.4). Pre-flight reassessed the audit's 'silently downgrades' claim: `Tensor::data()` (ferrotorch-core/src/tensor.rs:635-638) already Errs with `GpuTensorNotAccessible` on CUDA inputs, so `apply_fused` is NOT a silent demote ‚Äî it fails-fast already. #1106 cleaned up the doc drift and replaced the generic `GpuTensorNotAccessible` with an explicit `NotImplementedOnCuda` guard.

This follow-up tracks the audit's *Pattern B* path (deferred): route CUDA-input `apply_fused` through `FusedChain::generate_ptx` + a real GPU runtime executor.

## Why this was deferred from #1106

Wiring a runtime PTX executor requires workspace-level `ferrotorch-jit` ‚Üî `ferrotorch-gpu` integration that's structurally infeasible in a leaf-crate audit-fix:

1. ferrotorch-jit's `Cargo.toml` has optional `cudarc` but does NOT depend on `ferrotorch-gpu` ‚Äî adding that dep is workspace-level architectural work, not a leaf-crate fix.
2. The existing PTX-loader pattern lives at `ferrotorch-gpu/src/module_cache.rs:74` (`ctx.load_module(Ptx::from_src(ptx_src))` + `module.load_function(kernel_name)`). Reusing it from `ferrotorch-jit` requires either adding ferrotorch-gpu as a dep, factoring out a shared 'jit-runtime' crate, or moving the dispatch into ferrotorch-gpu and inverting the dep direction.
3. `codegen.rs:987-1017` already correctly returns `JitError::GpuBackendUnavailable` for `GpuCuda`/`GpuPtx` targets ‚Äî the same blocker.

## Scope estimate

- ~500 LoC across ferrotorch-jit + ferrotorch-gpu (or new shared crate)
- New dep + test infra (CUDA-having CI runner, or cfg-gated tests with hardware probe)
- Workspace-level decision on the dep direction (jit‚Üígpu vs. gpu‚Üíjit vs. shared crate)

## Acceptance for this follow-up

- `apply_fused` on a CUDA tensor dispatches through `FusedChain::generate_ptx` and the wired runtime executor, returning a CUDA tensor (matching device, no CPU readback).
- Discriminating test on a CUDA-having runner: input `is_cuda()` ‚áí output `is_cuda()`.
- ferrotorch-jit `compile()` for `GpuCuda`/`GpuPtx` targets honors the target instead of returning `GpuBackendUnavailable`.

## References

- #1106 ‚Äî fail-fast doc/error cleanup (this follow-up's parent leaf-fix)
- #982 ‚Äî umbrella audit
- ferrotorch-gpu/src/module_cache.rs:74 ‚Äî existing PTX loader pattern
- ferrotorch-jit/src/fusion.rs:260 ‚Äî `FusedChain::generate_ptx` (codegen, no runtime)
- ferrotorch-jit/src/codegen.rs:987-1017 ‚Äî STALE-NO-OP CUDA target arm
